NKJV.1: The wicked flee when no one pursues, But the righteous are bold as a lion.
NKJV.2: Because of the transgression of a land, many are its princes; But by a man of understanding and knowledge Right will be prolonged.
NKJV.3: A poor man who oppresses the poor Is like a driving rain which leaves no food.
NKJV.4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked,  But such as keep the law contend with them.
NKJV.5: Evil men do not understand justice, But those who seek the LORD understand all.
NKJV.6: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity Than one perverse in his ways, though he be rich.
NKJV.7: Whoever keeps the law is a discerning son, But a companion of gluttons shames his father.
NKJV.8: One who increases his possessions by usury and extortion Gathers it for him who will pity the poor.
NKJV.9: One who turns away his ear from hearing the law, Even his prayer is an abomination.
NKJV.10: Whoever causes the upright to go astray in an evil way, He himself will fall into his own pit;  But the blameless will inherit good.
NKJV.11: The rich man is wise in his own eyes, But the poor who has understanding searches him out.
NKJV.12: When the righteous rejoice, there is great glory; But when the wicked arise, men hide themselves.
NKJV.13: He who covers his sins will not prosper, But whoever confesses and forsakes them will have mercy.
NKJV.14: Happy is the man who is always reverent, But he who hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
NKJV.15: Like a roaring lion and a charging bear Is a wicked ruler over poor people.
NKJV.16: A ruler who lacks understanding is a great oppressor, But he who hates covetousness will prolong his days.
NKJV.17: A man burdened with bloodshed will flee into a pit; Let no one help him.
NKJV.18: Whoever walks blamelessly will be saved, But he who is perverse in his ways will suddenly fall.
NKJV.19: He who tills his land will have plenty of bread, But he who follows frivolity will have poverty enough!
NKJV.20: A faithful man will abound with blessings,  But he who hastens to be rich will not go unpunished.
NKJV.21: To show partiality is not good, Because for a piece of bread a man will transgress.
NKJV.22: A man with an evil eye hastens after riches, And does not consider that poverty will come upon him.
NKJV.23: He who rebukes a man will find more favor afterward Than he who flatters with the tongue.
NKJV.24: Whoever robs his father or his mother, And says, “It is no transgression,” The same is companion to a destroyer.
NKJV.25: He who is of a proud heart stirs up strife,  But he who trusts in the LORD will be prospered.
NKJV.26: He who trusts in his own heart is a fool, But whoever walks wisely will be delivered.
NKJV.27: He who gives to the poor will not lack, But he who hides his eyes will have many curses.
NKJV.28: When the wicked arise, men hide themselves; But when they perish, the righteous increase.

ESV.1: The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are bold as a lion.
ESV.2: When a land transgresses, it has many rulers, but with a man of understanding and knowledge, its stability will long continue.
ESV.3: A poor man who oppresses the poor is a beating rain that leaves no food.
ESV.4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but those who keep the law strive against them.
ESV.5: Evil men do not understand justice, but those who seek the LORD understand it completely.
ESV.6: Better is a poor man who walks in his integrity than a rich man who is crooked in his ways.
ESV.7: The one who keeps the law is a son with understanding, but a companion of gluttons shames his father.
ESV.8: Whoever multiplies his wealth by interest and profit  gathers it for him who is generous to the poor.
ESV.9: If one turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.
ESV.10: Whoever misleads the upright into an evil way will fall into his own pit, but the blameless will have a goodly inheritance.
ESV.11: A rich man is wise in his own eyes, but a poor man who has understanding will find him out.
ESV.12: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory, but when the wicked rise, people hide themselves.
ESV.13: Whoever conceals his transgressions will not prosper, but he who confesses and forsakes them will obtain mercy.
ESV.14: Blessed is the one who fears the LORD  always, but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
ESV.15: Like a roaring lion or a charging bear is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
ESV.16: A ruler who lacks understanding is a cruel oppressor, but he who hates unjust gain will prolong his days.
ESV.17: If one is burdened with the blood of another, he will be a fugitive until death;  let no one help him.
ESV.18: Whoever walks in integrity will be delivered, but he who is crooked in his ways will suddenly fall.
ESV.19: Whoever works his land will have plenty of bread, but he who follows worthless pursuits will have plenty of poverty.
ESV.20: A faithful man will abound with blessings, but whoever hastens to be rich will not go unpunished.
ESV.21: To show partiality is not good, but for a piece of bread a man will do wrong.
ESV.22: A stingy man  hastens after wealth and does not know that poverty will come upon him.
ESV.23: Whoever rebukes a man will afterward find more favor than he who flatters with his tongue.
ESV.24: Whoever robs his father or his mother and says, “That is no transgression,” is a companion to a man who destroys.
ESV.25: A greedy man stirs up strife, but the one who trusts in the LORD will be enriched.
ESV.26: Whoever trusts in his own mind is a fool, but he who walks in wisdom will be delivered.
ESV.27: Whoever gives to the poor will not want, but he who hides his eyes will get many a curse.
ESV.28: When the wicked rise, people hide themselves, but when they perish, the righteous increase.

NASB.1: The wicked flee when no one is pursuing, But the righteous are bold as a lion.
NASB.2: By the transgression of a land many are its princes, But by a man of understanding [and] knowledge, so it endures.
NASB.3: A poor man who oppresses the lowly Is [like] a driving rain which leaves no food.
NASB.4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, But those who keep the law strive with them.
NASB.5: Evil men do not understand justice, But those who seek the LORD understand all things.
NASB.6: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity Than he who is crooked though he be rich.
NASB.7: He who keeps the law is a discerning son, But he who is a companion of gluttons humiliates his father.
NASB.8: He who increases his wealth by interest and usury Gathers it for him who is gracious to the poor.
NASB.9: He who turns away his ear from listening to the law, Even his prayer is an abomination.
NASB.10: He who leads the upright astray in an evil way Will himself fall into his own pit, But the blameless will inherit good.
NASB.11: The rich man is wise in his own eyes, But the poor who has understanding sees through him.
NASB.12: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory, But when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.
NASB.13: He who conceals his transgressions will not prosper, But he who confesses and forsakes [them] will find compassion.
NASB.14: How blessed is the man who fears always, But he who hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
NASB.15: [Like] a roaring lion and a rushing bear Is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
NASB.16: A leader who is a great oppressor lacks understanding, [But] he who hates unjust gain will prolong [his] days.
NASB.17: A man who is laden with the guilt of human blood Will be a fugitive until death; let no one support him.
NASB.18: He who walks blamelessly will be delivered, But he who is crooked will fall all at once.
NASB.19: He who tills his land will have plenty of food, But he who follows empty [pursuits] will have poverty in plenty.
NASB.20: A faithful man will abound with blessings, But he who makes haste to be rich will not go unpunished.
NASB.21: To show partiality is not good, Because for a piece of bread a man will transgress.
NASB.22: A man with an evil eye hastens after wealth And does not know that want will come upon him.
NASB.23: He who rebukes a man will afterward find [more] favor Than he who flatters with the tongue.
NASB.24: He who robs his father or his mother And says, 'It is not a transgression,' Is the companion of a man who destroys.
NASB.25: An arrogant man stirs up strife, But he who trusts in the LORD will prosper.
NASB.26: He who trusts in his own heart is a fool, But he who walks wisely will be delivered.
NASB.27: He who gives to the poor will never want, But he who shuts his eyes will have many curses.
NASB.28: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves; But when they perish, the righteous increase.

NLT.1:
The wicked run away when no one is chasing them,
but the godly are as bold as lions.
NLT.2:
When there is moral rot within a nation, its government topples easily.
But wise and knowledgeable leaders bring stability.
NLT.3:
A poor person who oppresses the poor
is like a pounding rain that destroys the crops.
NLT.4:
To reject the law is to praise the wicked;
to obey the law is to fight them.
NLT.5:
Evil people don’t understand justice,
but those who follow the LORD understand completely.
NLT.6:
Better to be poor and honest
than to be dishonest and rich.
NLT.7:
Young people who obey the law are wise;
those with wild friends bring shame to their parents.
NLT.8:
Income from charging high interest rates
will end up in the pocket of someone who is kind to the poor.
NLT.9:
God detests the prayers
of a person who ignores the law.
NLT.10:
Those who lead good people along an evil path
will fall into their own trap,
but the honest will inherit good things.
NLT.11:
Rich people may think they are wise,
but a poor person with discernment can see right through them.
NLT.12:
When the godly succeed, everyone is glad.
When the wicked take charge, people go into hiding.
NLT.13:
People who conceal their sins will not prosper,
but if they confess and turn from them, they will receive mercy.
NLT.14:
Blessed are those who fear to do wrong,
but the stubborn are headed for serious trouble.
NLT.15:
A wicked ruler is as dangerous to the poor
as a roaring lion or an attacking bear.
NLT.16:
A ruler with no understanding will oppress his people,
but one who hates corruption will have a long life.
NLT.17:
A murderer’s tormented conscience will drive him into the grave.
Don’t protect him!
NLT.18:
The blameless will be rescued from harm,
but the crooked will be suddenly destroyed.
NLT.19:
A hard worker has plenty of food,
but a person who chases fantasies ends up in poverty.
NLT.20:
The trustworthy person will get a rich reward,
but a person who wants quick riches will get into trouble.
NLT.21:
Showing partiality is never good,
yet some will do wrong for a mere piece of bread.
NLT.22:
Greedy people try to get rich quick
but don’t realize they’re headed for poverty.
NLT.23:
In the end, people appreciate honest criticism
far more than flattery.
NLT.24:
Anyone who steals from his father and mother
and says, “What’s wrong with that?”
is no better than a murderer.
NLT.25:
Greed causes fighting;
trusting the LORD leads to prosperity.
NLT.26:
Those who trust their own insight are foolish,
but anyone who walks in wisdom is safe.
NLT.27:
Whoever gives to the poor will lack nothing,
but those who close their eyes to poverty will be cursed.
NLT.28:
When the wicked take charge, people go into hiding.
When the wicked meet disaster, the godly flourish.

WEB.1: The wicked flee when no one pursues; but the righteous are as bold as a lion.
WEB.2: In rebellion, a land has many rulers, but order is maintained by a man of understanding and knowledge.
WEB.3: A needy man who oppresses the poor is like a driving rain which leaves no crops.
WEB.4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked; but those who keep the law contend with them.
WEB.5: Evil men don’t understand justice; but those who seek Yahweh understand it fully.
WEB.6: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity, than he who is perverse in his ways, and he is rich.
WEB.7: Whoever keeps the law is a wise son; but he who is a companion of gluttons shames his father.
WEB.8: He who increases his wealth by excessive interest gathers it for one who has pity on the poor.
WEB.9: He who turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.
WEB.10: Whoever causes the upright to go astray in an evil way, he will fall into his own trap; but the blameless will inherit good.
WEB.11: The rich man is wise in his own eyes; but the poor who has understanding sees through him.
WEB.12: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory; but when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.
WEB.13: He who conceals his sins doesn’t prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
WEB.14: Blessed is the man who always fears; but one who hardens his heart falls into trouble.
WEB.15: As a roaring lion or a charging bear, so is a wicked ruler over helpless people.
WEB.16: A tyrannical ruler lacks judgment. One who hates ill-gotten gain will have long days.
WEB.17: A man who is tormented by life blood will be a fugitive until death; no one will support him.
WEB.18: Whoever walks blamelessly is kept safe; but one with perverse ways will fall suddenly.
WEB.19: One who works his land will have an abundance of food; but one who chases fantasies will have his fill of poverty.
WEB.20: A faithful man is rich with blessings; but one who is eager to be rich will not go unpunished.
WEB.21: To show partiality is not good; yet a man will do wrong for a piece of bread.
WEB.22: A stingy man hurries after riches, and doesn’t know that poverty waits for him.
WEB.23: One who rebukes a man will afterward find more favor than one who flatters with the tongue.
WEB.24: Whoever robs his father or his mother, and says, “It’s not wrong.” He is a partner with a destroyer.
WEB.25: One who is greedy stirs up strife; but one who trusts in Yahweh will prosper.
WEB.26: One who trusts in himself is a fool; but one who walks in wisdom is kept safe.
WEB.27: One who gives to the poor has no lack; but one who closes his eyes will have many curses.
WEB.28: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous thrive.

CSB17.1:
The wicked flee when no one is pursuing them, ⓙ
but the righteous are as bold as a lion. ⓚ
CSB17.2:
When a land is in rebellion, it has many rulers, ⓛ
but with a discerning and knowledgeable person, it endures.

is like a driving rain that leaves no food. ⓜ

The evil do not understand justice, ⓑ

but a companion of gluttons humiliates his father. ⓕ

collects it for one who is kind to the poor. ⓗ

will fall into his own pit, ⓙ

CSB17.11:

but a poor one who has discernment sees through him.
CSB17.12:
When the righteous triumph,
there is great rejoicing, [3]

people hide. ⓛ

The one who conceals his sins
will not prosper, ⓜ

will find mercy. ⓝ
CSB17.14:
Happy is the one who is always reverent, ⓐ
but one who hardens his heart falls into trouble. ⓑ
CSB17.15:
A wicked ruler over a helpless people ⓒ
is like a roaring lion or a charging bear. ⓓ
CSB17.16:
A leader who lacks understanding
is very oppressive,
but one who hates dishonest profit
prolongs his life. ⓔ
CSB17.17:
Someone burdened by bloodguilt [4]
will be a fugitive until death. ⓕ
Let no one help him. ⓖ
CSB17.18:
The one who lives with integrity will be helped,
but one who distorts right and wrong [5]
will suddenly fall. ⓗ
CSB17.19:
The one who works his land
will have plenty of food, ⓘ
but whoever chases fantasies
will have his fill of poverty. ⓙ

will not go unpunished.
CSB17.21:
It is not good to show partiality ⓜ  —
yet even a courageous person may sin for a piece of bread. ⓝ
CSB17.22:
A greedy one [6] is in a hurry for wealth;
he doesn’t know that poverty will come to him. ⓐ
CSB17.23:
One who rebukes a person will later find more favor ⓑ
than one who flatters [7] with his tongue. ⓒ
CSB17.24:
The one who robs his father or mother ⓓ
and says, “That’s no sin,” ⓔ
is a companion to a person who destroys. ⓕ
CSB17.25:
A greedy person stirs up conflict, ⓖ
but whoever trusts in the LORD will prosper. ⓗ
CSB17.26:
The one who trusts in himself [8] is a fool,
but one who walks in wisdom will be safe. ⓘ
CSB17.27:
The one who gives to the poor
will not be in need,
but one who turns his eyes away [9]
will receive many curses. ⓙ
CSB17.28:
When the wicked come to power,
people hide,
but when they are destroyed,
the righteous flourish. ⓚ

AMP.1:
The wicked flee when no one pursues them,
But the righteous are as bold as a lion.
AMP.2:
When a land does wrong, it has many princes,
But when the ruler is a man of understanding and knowledge, its stability endures.
AMP.3:
A poor man who oppresses and exploits the lowly
Is like a sweeping rain which leaves no food.
AMP.4:
Those who set aside the law [of God and man] praise the wicked,
But those who keep the law [of God and man] struggle with them.
AMP.5:
Evil men do not understand justice,
But they who long for and seek the LORD understand it fully.
AMP.6:
Better is the poor who walks in his integrity
Than he who is crooked and two-faced though he is rich.
AMP.7:
He who keeps the law [of God and man] is a wise and discerning son,
But he who is a companion of gluttons humiliates his father [and himself].
AMP.8:
He who increases his wealth by interest and usury (excessive interest)
Gathers it for him who is gracious to the poor.
AMP.9:
He who turns his ear away from listening to the law [of God and man],
Even his prayer is repulsive [to God].
AMP.10:
He who leads the upright astray on an evil path
Will himself fall into his own pit,
But the blameless will inherit good.
AMP.11:
The rich man [who is conceited and relies on his wealth instead of God] is wise in his own eyes,
But the poor man who has understanding [because he relies on God] is able to see through him.
AMP.12:
When the righteous triumph, there is great glory and celebration;
But when the wicked rise [to prominence], men hide themselves.
AMP.13:
He who conceals his transgressions will not prosper,
But whoever confesses and turns away from his sins will find compassion and mercy.
AMP.14:
Blessed and favored by God is the man who fears [sin and its consequence] at all times,
But he who hardens his heart [and is determined to sin] will fall into disaster.
AMP.15:
Like a roaring lion and a charging bear
Is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
AMP.16:
A leader who is a great oppressor lacks understanding and common sense [and his wickedness shortens his days],
But he who hates unjust gain will [be blessed and] prolong his days.
AMP.17:
A man who is burdened with the guilt of human blood (murder)
Will be a fugitive until death; let no one support him or give him refuge.
AMP.18:
He who walks blamelessly and uprightly will be kept safe,
But he who is crooked (perverse) will suddenly fall.
AMP.19:
He who cultivates his land will have plenty of bread,
But he who follows worthless people and frivolous pursuits will have plenty of poverty.
AMP.20:
A faithful (right-minded) man will abound with blessings,
But he who hurries to be rich will not go unpunished.
AMP.21:
To have regard for one person over another and to show favoritism is not good,
Because for a piece of bread a man will transgress.
AMP.22:
He who has an evil and envious eye hurries to be rich
And does not know that poverty will come upon him.
AMP.23:
He who [appropriately] reprimands a [wise] man will afterward find more favor
Than he who flatters with the tongue.
AMP.24:
He who robs his father or his mother
And says, "This is no sin,"
Is [not only a thief but also] the companion of a man who destroys.
AMP.25:
An arrogant and greedy man stirs up strife,
But he who trusts in the LORD will be blessed and prosper.
AMP.26:
He who trusts confidently in his own heart is a [dull, thickheaded] fool,
But he who walks in [skillful and godly] wisdom will be rescued.
AMP.27:
He who gives to the poor will never want,
But he who shuts his eyes [from their need] will have many curses.
AMP.28:
When the wicked rise [to power], men hide themselves;
But when the wicked perish, the [consistently] righteous increase and become great.

MSG.1:
The wicked are edgy with guilt, ready to run off
even when no one's after them;
Honest people are relaxed and confident,
bold as lions.
MSG.2:
When the country is in chaos,
everybody has a plan to fix it—
But it takes a leader of real understanding
to straighten things out.
MSG.3:
The wicked who oppress the poor
are like a hailstorm that beats down the harvest.
MSG.4:
If you desert God's law, you're free to embrace depravity;
if you love God's law, you fight for it tooth and nail.
MSG.5:
Justice makes no sense to the evilminded;
those who seek God know it inside and out.
MSG.6:
It's better to be poor and direct
than rich and crooked.
MSG.7:
Practice God's law—get a reputation for wisdom;
hang out with a loose crowd—embarrass your family.
MSG.8:
Get as rich as you want
through cheating and extortion,
But eventually some friend of the poor
is going to give it all back to them.
MSG.9:
God has no use for the prayers
of the people who won't listen to him.
MSG.10:
Lead good people down a wrong path
and you'll come to a bad end;
do good and you'll be rewarded for it.
MSG.11:
The rich think they know it all,
but the poor can see right through them.
MSG.12:
When good people are promoted, everything is great,
but when the bad are in charge, watch out!
MSG.13:
You can't whitewash your sins and get by with it;
you find mercy by admitting and leaving them.
MSG.14:
A tenderhearted person lives a blessed life;
a hardhearted person lives a hard life.
MSG.15:
Lions roar and bears charge—
and the wicked lord it over the poor.
MSG.16:
Among leaders who lack insight, abuse abounds,
but for one who hates corruption, the future is bright.
MSG.17:
A murderer haunted by guilt
is doomed—there's no helping him.
MSG.18:
Walk straight—live well and be saved;
a devious life is a doomed life.
MSG.19:
Work your garden—you'll end up with plenty of food;
play and party—you'll end up with an empty plate.
MSG.20:
Committed and persistent work pays off;
get-rich-quick schemes are ripoffs.
MSG.21:
Playing favorites is always a bad thing;
you can do great harm in seemingly harmless ways.
MSG.22:
A miser in a hurry to get rich
doesn't know that he'll end up broke.
MSG.23:
In the end, serious reprimand is appreciated
far more than bootlicking flattery.
MSG.24:
Anyone who robs father and mother
and says, "So, what's wrong with that?"
is worse than a pirate.
MSG.25:
A grasping person stirs up trouble,
but trust in God brings a sense of well-being.
MSG.26:
If you think you know it all, you're a fool for sure;
real survivors learn wisdom from others.
MSG.27:
Be generous to the poor—you'll never go hungry;
shut your eyes to their needs, and run a gauntlet of curses.
MSG.28:
When corruption takes over, good people go underground,
but when the crooks are thrown out, it's safe to come out.

NIV.1:
The wicked man flees though no one pursues,
but the righteous are as bold as a lion.
NIV.2:
When a country is rebellious, it has many rulers,
but a man of understanding and knowledge maintains order.
NIV.3:
A ruler who oppresses the poor
is like a driving rain that leaves no crops.
NIV.4:
Those who forsake the law praise the wicked,
but those who keep the law resist them.
NIV.5:
Evil men do not understand justice,
but those who seek the Lord understand it fully.
NIV.6:
Better a poor man whose walk is blameless
than a rich man whose ways are perverse.
NIV.7:
He who keeps the law is a discerning son,
but a companion of gluttons disgraces his father.
NIV.8:
He who increases his wealth by exorbitant interest
amasses it for another, who will be kind to the poor.
NIV.9:
If anyone turns a deaf ear to the law,
even his prayers are detestable.
NIV.10:
He who leads the upright along an evil path
will fall into his own trap,
but the blameless will receive a good inheritance.
NIV.11:
A rich man may be wise in his own eyes,
but a poor man who has discernment sees through him.
NIV.12:
When the righteous triumph, there is great elation;
but when the wicked rise to power, men go into hiding.
NIV.13:
He who conceals his sins does not prosper,
but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
NIV.14:
Blessed is the man who always fears the Lord,
but he who hardens his heart falls into trouble.
NIV.15:
Like a roaring lion or a charging bear
is a wicked man ruling over a helpless people.
NIV.16:
A tyrannical ruler lacks judgment,
but he who hates ill-gotten gain will enjoy a long life.
NIV.17:
A man tormented by the guilt of murder
will be a fugitive till death;
let no one support him.
NIV.18:
He whose walk is blameless is kept safe,
but he whose ways are perverse will suddenly fall.
NIV.19:
He who works his land will have abundant food,
but the one who chases fantasies will have his fill of poverty.
NIV.20:
A faithful man will be richly blessed,
but one eager to get rich will not go unpunished.
NIV.21:
To show partiality is not good—
yet a man will do wrong for a piece of bread.
NIV.22:
A stingy man is eager to get rich
and is unaware that poverty awaits him.
NIV.23:
He who rebukes a man will in the end gain more favor
than he who has a flattering tongue.
NIV.24:
He who robs his father or mother
and says, “It’s not wrong”—
he is partner to him who destroys.
NIV.25:
A greedy man stirs up dissension,
but he who trusts in the Lord will prosper.
NIV.26:
He who trusts in himself is a fool,
but he who walks in wisdom is kept safe.
NIV.27:
He who gives to the poor will lack nothing,
but he who closes his eyes to them receives many curses.
NIV.28:
When the wicked rise to power, people go into hiding;
but when the wicked perish, the righteous thrive.

NRSVCE.1:
The wicked flee when no one pursues,
but the righteous are as bold as a lion.
NRSVCE.2:
When a land rebels
it has many rulers;
but with an intelligent ruler
there is lasting order.
NRSVCE.3:
A ruler who oppresses the poor
is a beating rain that leaves no food.
NRSVCE.4:
Those who forsake the law praise the wicked,
but those who keep the law struggle against them.
NRSVCE.5:
The evil do not understand justice,
but those who seek the LORD understand it completely.
NRSVCE.6:
Better to be poor and walk in integrity
than to be crooked in one’s ways even though rich.
NRSVCE.7:
Those who keep the law are wise children,
but companions of gluttons shame their parents.
NRSVCE.8:
One who augments wealth by exorbitant interest
gathers it for another who is kind to the poor.
NRSVCE.9:
When one will not listen to the law,
even one’s prayers are an abomination.
NRSVCE.10:
Those who mislead the upright into evil ways
will fall into pits of their own making,
but the blameless will have a goodly inheritance.
NRSVCE.11:
The rich is wise in self-esteem,
but an intelligent poor person sees through the pose.
NRSVCE.12:
When the righteous triumph, there is great glory,
but when the wicked prevail, people go into hiding.
NRSVCE.13:
No one who conceals transgressions will prosper,
but one who confesses and forsakes them will obtain mercy.
NRSVCE.14:
Happy is the one who is never without fear,
but one who is hard-hearted will fall into calamity.
NRSVCE.15:
Like a roaring lion or a charging bear
is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
NRSVCE.16:
A ruler who lacks understanding is a cruel oppressor;
but one who hates unjust gain will enjoy a long life.
NRSVCE.17:
If someone is burdened with the blood of another,
let that killer be a fugitive until death;
let no one offer assistance.
NRSVCE.18:
One who walks in integrity will be safe,
but whoever follows crooked ways will fall into the Pit.
NRSVCE.19:
Anyone who tills the land will have plenty of bread,
but one who follows worthless pursuits will have plenty of poverty.
NRSVCE.20:
The faithful will abound with blessings,
but one who is in a hurry to be rich will not go unpunished.
NRSVCE.21:
To show partiality is not good —
yet for a piece of bread a person may do wrong.
NRSVCE.22:
The miser is in a hurry to get rich
and does not know that loss is sure to come.
NRSVCE.23:
Whoever rebukes a person will afterward find more favor
than one who flatters with the tongue.
NRSVCE.24:
Anyone who robs father or mother
and says, “That is no crime,”
is partner to a thug.
NRSVCE.25:
The greedy person stirs up strife,
but whoever trusts in the LORD will be enriched.
NRSVCE.26:
Those who trust in their own wits are fools;
but those who walk in wisdom come through safely.
NRSVCE.27:
Whoever gives to the poor will lack nothing,
but one who turns a blind eye will get many a curse.
NRSVCE.28:
When the wicked prevail, people go into hiding;
but when they perish, the righteous increase.

NET.1: The wicked person flees when there is no one pursuing, but the righteous person is as confident as a lion.
NET.2: When a country is rebellious it has many princes, but by someone who is discerning and knowledgeable order is maintained.
NET.3: A poor person who oppresses the weak is like a driving rain without food.
NET.4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but those who keep the law contend with them.
NET.5: Evil people do not understand justice, but those who seek the LORD understand it all.
NET.6: A poor person who walks in his integrity is better than one who is perverse in his ways even though he is rich.
NET.7: The one who keeps the law is a discerning child, but a companion of gluttons brings shame to his parents.
NET.8: The one who increases his wealth by increasing interest gathers it for someone who is gracious to the needy.
NET.9: The one who turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.
NET.10: The one who leads the upright astray in an evil way will himself fall into his own pit, but the blameless will inherit what is good.
NET.11: A rich person is wise in his own eyes, but a discerning poor person can evaluate him properly.
NET.12: When the righteous rejoice, great is the glory, but when the wicked rise to power, people are sought out.
NET.13: The one who covers his transgressions will not prosper, but whoever confesses them and forsakes them will find mercy.
NET.14: Blessed is the one who is always cautious, but whoever hardens his heart will fall into evil.
NET.15: Like a roaring lion or a roving bear, so is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
NET.16: The prince who is a great oppressor lacks wisdom, but the one who hates unjust gain will prolong his days.
NET.17: The one who is tormented by the murder of another will flee to the pit; let no one support him.
NET.18: The one who walks blamelessly will be delivered, but whoever is perverse in his ways will fall at once.
NET.19: The one who works his land will be satisfied with food, but whoever chases daydreams will have his fill of poverty.
NET.20: A faithful person will have an abundance of blessings, but the one who hastens to gain riches will not go unpunished.
NET.21: To show partiality is terrible, for a person will transgress over the smallest piece of bread.
NET.22: The stingy person hastens after riches and does not know that poverty will overtake him.
NET.23: The one who reproves another will in the end find more favor than the one who flatters with the tongue.
NET.24: The one who robs his father and mother and says, “There is no transgression,” is a companion to the one who destroys.
NET.25: The greedy person stirs up dissension, but the one who trusts in the LORD will prosper.
NET.26: The one who trusts in his own heart is a fool, but the one who walks in wisdom will escape.
NET.27: The one who gives to the poor will not lack, but whoever shuts his eyes to them will receive many curses.
NET.28: When the wicked gain control, people hide themselves, but when they perish, the righteous increase.
